# ArcFlow

> The operational world model layer — the persistence tier for actual world state. Neural world models simulate; ArcFlow records. Spatial-temporal, confidence-scored, embedded. Runs in-process: browser (WASM), Node.js, Python, Rust, native CLI. No server, no Docker — the engine is 5MB and runs inside your process. Space and time are first-class dimensions; every fact carries an observation class (observed/inferred/predicted) and confidence score.
>
> Eight engines, one query language. Graph storage, query execution, live streaming views, event bus, behavior engine, algorithm library, durability, and language bindings — pre-integrated. One ISO GQL dialect describes the schema, the query, the live view, the trigger, and the algorithm call. Sub-millisecond latency is the consequence of not assembling separate stacks.

## Try instantly (zero install)

Open https://oz.com/engine in your browser. Paste any query. Persistent storage.

## Install

The current shipping install matrix is at:
https://oz.com/docs/installation
Source: https://github.com/ozinc/arcflow/releases/latest/download/release-matrix.json

Currently shipped:
  curl -fsSL https://staging.oz.com/install/arcflow | sh                                # native CLI
The Python, Node, and Rust bindings are planned (RAM-C2 / 2026-Q3) —
see the matrix above for current status. ArcFlow does not ship a Docker image for the engine
itself (5MB embedded library; Docker would subvert the in-process design).
See https://oz.com/docs/deployment/docker for the rationale.

## 5 essential patterns

```typescript
import { open, openInMemory, ArcflowError } from '@ozinc/arcflow'

// 1. World model entity — spatial position, observation class, confidence
const db = openInMemory()
db.mutate("CREATE (e:Entity {name: 'Unit-01', x: 12.4, y: 8.7, _observation_class: 'observed', _confidence: 0.97})")
db.query("CALL algo.nearestNodes(point({x: 0, y: 0}), 'Entity', 5) YIELD node, distance RETURN node.name, distance")

// 2. Parameters (prevents injection)
db.query("MATCH (e {id: $id}) RETURN e", { id: 'unit-01' })

// 3. Algorithms (no projection setup)
db.query("CALL algo.pageRank()")

// 4. Vector search (built-in, no separate service)
db.mutate("CREATE VECTOR INDEX idx FOR (n:Doc) ON (n.embedding) OPTIONS {dimensions: 1536, similarity: 'cosine'}")
db.query("CALL algo.vectorSearch('idx', $vec, 10)", { vec: JSON.stringify([0.1, 0.2]) })

// 5. Persistence (WAL-journaled, survives crashes)
const persistent = open('./data/graph')
```

## What ArcFlow replaces (one engine)

- Graph database server → in-process, no server, `openInMemory()` for testing
- Separate spatial system → R*-tree built in, `CALL algo.nearestNodes()`
- Time-series store → every mutation versioned, `AS OF seq N` on the same graph
- Vector database → HNSW built in, `CALL algo.vectorSearch()`
- Streaming broker → `CREATE LIVE VIEW`, `db.subscribe()`, CDC built in
- Workflow orchestrator → `CALL arcflow.workflow.create/run/retryStep()`
- LLM orchestration → `CREATE SKILL ... TIER LLM MODEL '...'` + `PROCESS NODE`; provider key vault + per-key daily caps in CLI
- Agent verification gate → `arcflow receipt generate` + `arcflow hook stop-check` (six-state machine, tamper-evident receipts)
- Plugin manager → `arcflow plugin install` (SHA-256 integrity, engine-compat check, air-gapped via `--from`)

## Architecture — eight layers (bottom → top)

ArcFlow is the hero — "the blazing-fast graph engine for modeling the real world." The eight layers are the engine's anatomy; World Graph (Layer 3) is the hero layer that carries the typed real-world entities. World Store is internal substrate, never a sellable SKU.

1. **World Store** — storage substrate (internal); Iceberg-shaped manifests, WAL segments, snapshots, parquet blocks; addressed by `lake://` URIs.
2. **Perception Lake** — append-only observation landing (Bronze tier; reserved — no substrate ships today); observation-time stamped, immutable.
3. **World Graph** ★ — typed entity layer (the hero layer); nodes / edges / indexes / catalog; mission tiers; HLC provenance; view *over* the World Store.
4. **Query Engine** — Cypher / ISO GQL parser + planner + executor; predicate pushdown across the storage layers via one schema.
5. **Live Surface** — standing queries + Z-set deltas; `CREATE LIVE VIEW`, `db.subscribe()`.
6. **Event Bus** — in-process pub/sub; topics, consumer groups, ack/nack, dead-letter.
7. **Behavior Engine** — declarative behaviors, triggers, durable workflows.
8. **Algorithm Library** — built-in graph + spatial + vector primitives; `CALL algo.*`.

The boundary between Layer 1 (World Store) and Layer 3 (World Graph) is a module boundary, not a process boundary — ArcFlow stays one in-process engine. Layer files: `docs/concepts/layers/<name>.mdx`.

## Agent integration surfaces (three, no overlap)

If you have a shell: use the `arcflow` CLI binary. Write a `.cypher` file, execute it, read the `.json` result. Zero protocol overhead, zero token cost. The filesystem is the API.

- **napi-rs in-process** (~1µs) — application daemons, broker processes
- **`arcflow` CLI binary** (<10ms) — Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, shell scripts, Python pipelines
- **MCP server** (~100ms) — cloud chat UIs only (Claude.ai, ChatGPT web — no local shell)

```bash
# Shell-native agents: write query, execute, read result
echo 'MATCH (n:Function)-[:CALLS]->(m) RETURN n.name, m.name' > query.cypher
arcflow --data-dir ./graph --exec query.cypher --json > result.json
cat result.json
```

## API

- `openInMemory()` → ArcflowDB
- `open(dataDir)` → ArcflowDB (persistent)
- `db.query(cypher, params?)` → { columns, rows, rowCount, computeMs, gqlstatus(), snapshotUri }
- `db.queryAt(snapshotUri, cypher, params?)` → TaggedQueryResult (provenanced replay)
- `db.mutate(cypher, params?)` → MutationResult
- `db.batchMutate(queries[])` → number
- `db.subscribe(query, handler, opts?)` → LiveQuery (cancel())
- `db.cursor(query, params?, pageSize?)` → QueryCursor
- `row.get('column')` → string | number | boolean | null (typed)
- `ArcflowError` → { code, category, suggestion }
- `@arcflow/react` → useQuery(db, q, params), useLiveQuery(db, q)

## Python surface (full)

Bulk ingest:
- `db.bulk_create_nodes([(labels, props), ...])` → list[NodeId]. CREATE semantics — use `MERGE` for find-or-create.
- `db.bulk_create_relationships(rel_type, [(from_id, to_id, props), ...])` → list[RelId].
- `db.bulk_create_nodes_from_arrow(label, pa.Table)` / `bulk_create_relationships_from_arrow(rel_type, pa.Table)` — Arrow-direct, no JSON marshal.
- `db.load_parquet(path, label, chunk_size=100_000)` / `db.load_csv(path, label)` — file-direct.

Virtual labels (Lakehouse-backed nodes):
- `CREATE NODE LABEL <name> [(col TYPE, ...)] VIRTUAL FROM PARTITION '<lake-uri>'` — DDL form. Rows live in the Lakehouse partition; the engine holds typed schema + adjacency + catalog pointer.
- `db.register_virtual_partition(label, partition)` → `int` epoch — direct registration without a Cypher round-trip. C ABI: `arcflow_register_virtual_partition`.
- Workspace addressing: `oz://workspace`, `oz://snapshot/<digest>`, `oz://label/<name>`, `oz://edge/<name>`, `oz://catalog`, `oz://partition/<digest>`.

Virtual computed columns:
- `CREATE NODE LABEL <name> VIRTUAL FROM PARTITION '<lake-uri>' COMPUTE <col1> = <expr>, <col2> = <expr>, ...` — derived properties declared on the virtual label. Evaluated by the Smart Reader at row-decode time against the decoded RecordBatch; never materialised to disk. Surface as entries in `Node.properties` alongside parquet-resident columns. Predicates on a computed column push down through the planner — partition + row-group pruning collapses the candidate set before evaluation.
- Expressions reference parquet-resident columns, partition-key variables (typed Int / String), and earlier computed columns (topologically ordered). Arithmetic, array indexing, `sqrt`, `abs`, `floor`, `ceil`, `pow`, and comparisons are Arrow-evaluable; graph traversals are not callable inside `COMPUTE`.
- Catalog manifest commits a `ComputedColumn { name, return_type, dependencies, expr_ir }` alongside the `VirtualLabelEntry`. Same atomic two-file commit protocol; monotonic epoch.

Partition-key column exposure:
- Hive-style partition keys in lakehouse URIs (`lake://prod/trades/year=2026/region=eu/`) surface as plain typed properties on every virtual-label node. `MATCH (t:Trade) WHERE t.year = 2026 AND t.region = 'eu' RETURN count(*)` walks only the matching subtree; other partitions are pruned before any parquet file is opened.
- `result.io_stats.partitions_pruned` reports how many partition directories were skipped; `partitions_scanned` reports how many were walked.

Hybrid search:
- `algo.hybridSearch($queryVec, label, k)` and `algo.graphRAGTrusted(...)` combine vector similarity with graph traversal in one call. `algo.vectorSearch(<index>, $vec, k)` is the pure-vector form. Node-based overloads: `algo.hybridSearch(source_node_id)` YIELD nodeId, score, hops; `algo.similarNodes(source_node_id, k)` YIELD nodeId, score (k defaults to 10, source auto-picked if omitted).
- `CALL arcflow.hybridIndex.register(name, label, vector_property[, text_property, embedder]) YIELD name` registers a hybrid-index policy; once a default is set with `arcflow.hybridIndex.setDefault(name)`, `algo.vectorSearch` consults both legs automatically. `arcflow.hybridIndex.list()` / `.drop(name)` round out the surface.

Geofences (live spatial triggers):
- `CALL arcflow.geofence.register(name, property_key, cx, cy, radius)` registers a named circular region attached to a node-property key. The engine emits standing-query deltas every time a node enters or leaves the region. `arcflow.geofence.list()` / `.drop(name)` round out the surface.

k-Hop neighborhood maintenance:
- `CALL arcflow.k_hop.compute(source_label, rel_type, k) YIELD source, neighbor, distance` precomputes and maintains the k-hop neighborhood index incrementally as the graph mutates. `arcflow.k_hop.list()` / `.drop(id)` round out the surface. Useful for recommendation, similarity, and graph-attention feature pipelines.

Query hints (explicit lane selection):
- `CALL algo.X(...) HINT lane=<ident> YIELD ...` — overrides the planner's automatic lane choice. Supported lanes: `auto | cpu | cuda | metal | gpu.cuda | gpu.metal`. The chosen lane is reported back as `result.transport_outcome.lane`. If the requested lane isn't available on the host, the engine falls back silently to the next-best lane and records the actual lane used — use the field to detect silent fallbacks in tests.

Account login (optional, for hosted features only):
- `arcflow login` — browser-based; opens `oz.com/world/login` and listens on a local callback port. `arcflow login --token <TOKEN>` for headless/CI mode. Credentials at `~/.arcflow/credentials.json` (0600 on Unix). `arcflow whoami` prints email + tier. `arcflow logout` clears.
- Local-only use does not require login. Login unlocks first-party hosted features (`oz/*` LLM provider catalog, hosted skill packs). Everything else — graph, queries, live views, BYOK LLM providers — works fully offline.

LLM Node (provider-pluggable, BYOK):
- CLI: `arcflow keys add <provider> [--from-stdin | --key SECRET]`, `arcflow keys list`, `arcflow keys rm <provider>`, `arcflow keys set-daily-cap <provider> <USD>`. Keys live in the OS keychain (macOS Keychain, Linux Secret Service, Windows Credential Manager) — never written to disk.
- Skill grammar: `CREATE SKILL name FROM PROMPT '...' [ALLOWED ON [Label, ...]] [TIER LLM] [MODEL '<catalog-row>']`. `MODEL` routes the LLM call to a specific catalog row instead of the default `oz/deepseek-v3`.
- Providers: `openai/*` (OpenAI-compatible HTTPS — OpenAI, Together, Groq, Anyscale, any same-shape API), `cli/*` (SubprocessCli — `cli/claude-code`, `cli/codex`, `cli/gemini`; zero network egress), `oz/*` (first-party catalog at `llm.oz.com`).
- Catalog + budget: `arcflow keys list` shows provider, masked_key, daily_cap_usd, and spent_today_usd. BudgetMeter intercepts every LLM call and rejects on cap-exceeded with a typed error.
- Architecture: LLM calls route through an `arcflow-llm` sidecar process supervised by the runtime — provider state isolated, crash-restarted by the supervisor, failures sandboxed from the engine.

Plugin system:
- CLI: `arcflow plugin install <NAME> [--from <tarball>]`, `uninstall <NAME>`, `list [--json]`, `verify <NAME>`. Plugins stored in `~/.arcflow/plugins/`. Install validates slug, downloads from GitHub Releases (or local tarball), verifies SHA-256 integrity and engine compatibility.
- Python: `from arcflow.plugins import install, uninstall, list_plugins, verify, status, info`. Pure CLI wrapper; works even without the Rust engine loaded.

Agent governance (receipts + hooks + state machine):
- State machine: `CLEAN → DIRTY_UNANALYZED → GRAPH_FRESH → IMPACT_KNOWN → TEST_PLAN_KNOWN → VERIFIED`. File edits from `GRAPH_FRESH` or beyond reset to `DIRTY_UNANALYZED`.
- Hooks: `arcflow hook pre-tool <TOOL_NAME>` (no-op in v1), `arcflow hook post-edit` (invalidates state on edit), `arcflow hook stop-check` (blocks agent stop unless `VERIFIED`).
- Receipts: `arcflow receipt generate --base <ref> --head <ref>`, `verify [--base --head]`, `show`, `list`. Sealed receipt with SHA-256 integrity hash; four verification layers (schema, hash, state, diff-hash freshness). Receipt IDs: `arc_rcpt_<16-hex>`.

Prediction drift and flywheel tuning:
- `CALL algo.predictionDrift(entity_id, horizon_ms)` — spatial drift between predicted and observed positions. 12 YIELD columns including delta_magnitude (Euclidean distance). `horizon_ms` defaults to 1000.
- `CALL algo.confidenceCalibration(entity_id, window)` — Expected Calibration Error (ECE). YIELD: ece_score, sample_count, calibration_curve, calibration_status. Window: `"1h"`, `"30m"`, `"24h"`.
- `CALL arcflow.flywheel.tune(query1, query2, ...)` — dry-run query analysis; YIELD action, rationale, bounded. Three passes: missing indexes, cache pressure, failure remediation.

Read:
- `db.execute(query, params=None)` → QueryResult.
- `db.prepare(query)` → PreparedStatement (parser cached; `.execute(params)` for replays).
- `db.query_at(query, seq)` → snapshot-pinned read.
- `db.subscribe(view_name)` → push-based live-view subscription.
- `db.changes_since(seq, limit)` → CDC pull stream.
- `db.fingerprint()` → graph content hash. `db.snapshot_uri()` → `arcflow://snapshot/<hex>`.

Result handoff:
- `result.to_arrow()` → `pyarrow.RecordBatch` (zero-copy via Arrow C Data Interface). Also `result.to_polars()`, `result.to_pandas()`. Types preserved end-to-end — `"42"` stays a `String`.

Concurrency:
- ctypes releases the GIL on every native call; `ConcurrentStore` is MVCC-safe. `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor` works out of the box.

Event bus (in-process pub/sub + request-reply):
- `arcflow.bus.Bus(db)` — topics, durable consumers, hierarchical wildcard subscriptions (`*` matches one token, `>` matches the tail), synchronous request-reply.
- `bus.publish(topic, data, headers=None)` / `bus.events_after(topic, after_seq=0)`.
- `bus.register_consumer(name, topic, start=START_FROM_BEGINNING)` + `bus.pending(...)` + `bus.ack(...)`.
- `bus.register_pattern_consumer(name, "all_tracks.*")` — wildcards; future matching topics auto-attach at LatestOnly.
- `bus.request(topic, payload, timeout_ms=5_000)` — synchronous request-reply; raises `ArcFlowError(REQUEST_TIMEOUT)` on deadline.
- `arcflow.bus.EventPublisher(bus)` — rate-limit + per-camera routing + severity-tagged topic prefixes.
- See `docs/event-bus.mdx` for the full semantics.

Repository ergonomics:
- `arcflow.repository.BaseRepository(data_dir=None, *, shared_db=None, default_timeout_s=5.0)` — drop-in shape for services migrating off Memgraph Bolt. `execute / execute_rows / execute_one / execute_scalar / transaction / run_in_transaction / bulk_create_*`. Per-call `timeout_s` enforced via thread-join deadline (raises `RepositoryTimeoutError`).

Streaming functions:
- `arcflow.stream_fn.StreamFn(db, name, Kind.Lag(n) | Kind.Lead(n) | Kind.Delta)` — per-feed scalar emissions for the per-step-derivative pattern.

## Snapshot-pinned reads (provenanced answers)

Every result envelope carries a snapshot URI: `arcflow://snapshot/<hex>` (content-addressed).

- SDK: `result.snapshotUri`; replay with `db.queryAt(uri, cypher, params?)`
- CLI: `--json` emits `__snapshot`; pin with `--at-snapshot <uri>` or `arcflow rerun --snapshot <uri> --query "..."`
- HTTP: response headers `X-Arcflow-Snapshot-Id`, `X-Arcflow-Manifest-Pin`; pin with `?at=<uri>`
- MCP: `read_query`/`graph_rag` envelopes carry `snapshot_id`; accept optional `snapshot_uri` input

See docs/concepts/snapshots.mdx.

## Filesystem projection (the agent-grep workflow)

`arcflow project <mountpoint>` writes the current snapshot to a deterministic directory tree:
nodes/<Label>/<id>.json, edges/<Type>/<id>.json, indexes/labels.txt, indexes/types.txt,
plus __snapshot.toml. Two projections of the same snapshot are byte-identical. `--json`
returns `{snapshot_uri, node_count, edge_count, layout_version}`. Agents reason about
the world model with `rg`, `grep`, `cat`, `jq` — no MCP, no Cypher. See docs/cli/project.mdx.

## GQL / WorldCypher (ISO/IEC 39075 GQL) — 100% openCypher TCK (3881/3881)

CREATE, MATCH, MERGE, SET, DELETE, DETACH DELETE, REMOVE,
WITH, UNWIND, WHERE, RETURN, ORDER BY, LIMIT,
OPTIONAL MATCH, AS OF, CONTAINS, STARTS WITH,
CREATE VECTOR INDEX, CREATE FULLTEXT INDEX,
CREATE CONSTRAINT ON :L(p) ASSERT UNIQUE,
CREATE CONSTRAINT ON :L(p) ASSERT PRIMARY KEY,
IS LABELED :Label, ELEMENT_ID(n),
RETURN * NEXT WHEN/THEN/ELSE/END,
START TRANSACTION [READ ONLY | READ WRITE], COMMIT, ROLLBACK,
CALL algo.pageRank(), CALL algo.louvain(), CALL algo.vectorSearch(),
CALL algo.nearestNodes(), CALL arcflow.scene.frustumQuery(), CALL spatial.raycast(),
CALL db.stats(), CALL db.schema(), CALL db.help(), CALL db.spatialMetadata(),
CALL db.gpuStatus(), CALL dbms.gpuThresholds(),
CALL db.storageMode(), CALL db.warmCsr(), CALL db.csrStats(), CALL db.denseStore(),
CALL db.parallelConfig(), CALL db.executionContext(), CALL db.otelPolicy(),
CALL db.auth.whoami(), CALL db.auth.policies(), CALL db.auth.createApiKey(),
CALL arcflow.session.open(), CALL arcflow.session.list(), CALL arcflow.session.close(),
CALL arcflow.workflow.create(), CALL arcflow.workflow.run(), CALL arcflow.workflow.cancel(),
CALL arcflow.workflow.retryStep(),
CALL arcflow.scene.toUsda(), CALL arcflow.scene.frustumQuery(), CALL arcflow.scene.lineOfSight(),
CALL arcflow.replication.contract(), CALL arcflow.replication.walTailing(),
CALL arcflow.skills.export(), CALL arcflow.skills.import(),
CALL arcflow.ontology.subtypes(), CALL arcflow.ontology.ancestors(),
CALL arcflow.causalLineage(), CALL arcflow.causalPath(),
CALL arcflow.multi_source_disagreement(),
CALL arcflow.trajectory.nearestAtFrame(), CALL arcflow.trajectory.leverageGain(),
CALL arcflow.trajectory.releasePoint(), CALL arcflow.trajectory.shadowedBy(),
CALL arcflow.counterfactual.branchAt(),
LIVE MATCH, LIVE CALL, CREATE LIVE VIEW

## PostgreSQL wire protocol (SQL tooling bridge)

```bash
arcflow --pg 5432          # connect psql, Grafana, DBeaver, pgAdmin, ORMs
```

Queries are WorldCypher (GQL), not SQL. The wire protocol is transport-level only.
See docs/guides/sql-to-gql.mdx for the full adoption path.

## Common errors

- `EXPECTED_KEYWORD` → query syntax wrong, check MATCH/CREATE/MERGE
- `UNKNOWN_PROCEDURE` → run `CALL db.procedures()` to see available

## Integration recipe

See docs/guides/agent-quickstart.mdx for the 10-step integration guide.

## Cookbook recipes (runnable, CI-tested)

Every recipe is a self-contained `examples/<slug>/` directory: `meta.toml` + `pyproject.toml` + numbered `.py` steps + sample data + README. CI-runs against the engine version pinned in meta.toml. Source: https://github.com/ozinc/arcflow/tree/main/examples

- **agent-knowledge-base** — agent memory via confidence-scored GraphRAG (python, agent, ml; ≤ 5 min)
- **algo-trading-world-model** — persistent memory + orchestrated context + evidence-algebra fusion (python, ml, quant; ≤ 3 min)
- **deadline-aware-query** — bounded wall-clock budget via `arcflow.QueryOptions(deadline_ms=N)` (python, data-engineer, ml, agent; ≤ 3 min)
- **fraud-graph-traversal** — fraud detection via graph traversal (python, compliance, ml; ≤ 5 min)
- **from-sql-to-arcflow** — DuckDB-vs-ArcFlow side-by-side for graph + temporal + spatial questions (python, sql-user; ≤ 1 min)
- **lakehouse-count-fast-path** — parquet footer scan for `count(:VirtualLabel)` at any scale (python, data-engineer; ≤ 3 min)
- **multi-stream-spatiotemporal-world-model** — 60 Hz live tracking + synced auxiliary streams (python, ml, agent; ≤ 8 min)
- **sensor-fusion-livequery** — multi-sensor fusion with trust-weighted live alerts (python, ml, data-engineer; ≤ 5 min)
- **spatiotemporal-tactical-queries** — AS OF counterfactuals + confidence-weighted entity resolution + observed-vs-predicted filters (python, ml, agent; ≤ 1 min)
- **team-sports-tactical-world-model** — pressing, line-breaking pass, defensive compression, counter-attack (python, sports-analytics, agent; ≤ 3 min)
- **temporal-counterfactual-replay** — AS OF seq for decision audit (python, compliance, agent; ≤ 1 min)
- **temporal-spatial-parquet-ingest** — temporal + spatial parquet ingestion (python, data-engineer, ml; ≤ 5 min)
- **virtual-labels-over-parquet** — Lakehouse fast-path; `CREATE NODE LABEL ... VIRTUAL FROM PARTITION 'lake://...'` + `COMPUTE` derived columns (python, data-engineer, ml; ≤ 5 min)

Cookbook index: https://staging.oz.com/docs/cookbooks-index

## Key guides (stable)

- docs/concepts/snapshots.mdx — snapshot URI scheme, query_at, --at-snapshot, arcflow rerun, MCP/HTTP envelopes
- docs/cli/project.mdx — arcflow project: deterministic on-disk layout, the agent-grep workflow
- docs/concepts/world-model.mdx — what a world model is: spatial-temporal, confidence-scored, observed/inferred/predicted
- docs/guides/world-model.mdx — step-by-step: entities in space, spatial queries, temporal replay, live monitoring
- docs/use-cases/autonomous-systems.mdx — robot fleets, UAVs, shared world model, multi-agent coordination
- docs/use-cases/digital-twins.mdx — live spatial replica of physical facilities, temporal history, anomaly detection
- docs/use-cases/robotics.mdx — sensor fusion pipeline, track lifecycle, ROS bridge, emergency-stop live safety monitoring
- docs/guides/swarm-agents.mdx — AI agent swarms on a shared world model, durable workflows, named sessions
- docs/guides/rag-pipeline.mdx — Trusted RAG: confidence-filtered retrieval, graph context, vector + graph + full-text
- docs/guides/data-quality.mdx — live DQ with CREATE LIVE VIEW, batch==delta equivalence, pipeline integrity
- docs/guides/sql-to-gql.mdx — PostgreSQL wire protocol: connect psql/DBeaver/Grafana, then migrate to native GQL
- docs/agent-native.mdx — the world model as files: CLI binary, filesystem workspace, batch execution, watch mode

## Full reference

See AGENTS.md for complete API + WorldCypher syntax.
See llms-full.txt for every procedure and pattern.
